LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND OFFICERS FULGADO AND HERNANDEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. THE CITY IS IMMUNE UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 845.8(B). GOVERNMENT CODE 845.8 IS TO BE APPLIED BROADLY. (SEE KISBEY VS. CALIFORNIA (1984) 36 CAL.3RD 415.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and OFFICERS FULGADO AND HERNANDEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICTION. Appearances: Andrew Klimenko, Esq. of the Dolan Law Firm (415) 421-2800 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Robert A. Bonta, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4268 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment granted. The City is immune under Government Code sections 845.8(b). Government Code 845.8 is to be applied broadly. (See Kisbey vs. California (1984) 36 Cal.3rd 415.) Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
